methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 18 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Periodic Labs is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Liam Fedus and Ekin Dogus Cubuk that has raised $300M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers (including Palantir, Waymo, Figure AI, Scale AI, and Applied Intuition) and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR), representing 18 total companies in the AI & Software sector.

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient granularity within the narrow peer set; while 18 same-sector companies exist, filtering for identical stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints, rendering a tight peer median unreliable. The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier—standard for progression from Seed to the next financing round—to the $300M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $750M.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $638M (low) to $862M (high), with a median of $750M. This range reflects the single applicable methodology and should be weighted accordingly. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for a company of this capital intensity, and the lack of granular peer comparables at the identical stage and valuation tier. Additional data on revenue, burn rate, or funding round pricing would strengthen conviction in this range.
